title: Flu Season 2

jokes.text: Martin's celebrity impressions performance - doing Quentin Tarantino, John Malkovich, Tommy Lee Jones, and George W. Bush in rapid succession
jokes.setup: Local talent audition for Unity Concert
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical celebrity impression mashup that makes no narrative sense
jokes.explanation: Random celebrity name-dropping without clear logic, but captures the awkwardness of bad local talent

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: I'm gonna do impressions of celebrities for the Unity Concert talent show.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown Interviewer
dialogue.line: Oh great, what impressions do you do?

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: *does completely nonsensical mashup of random celebrity impressions that makes no sense*

jokes.text: "Who are any of those people?"
jokes.punchline: Complete dismissal revealing the impressions were meaningless
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan deflation of the previous nonsense. Good economy and timing.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown Interviewer
dialogue.line: Those were impressive impressions, but what was the point?

dialogue.speaker: Martin
dialogue.line: The point? There is no point. I just do voices.

jokes.text: Leslie's obsessive task completion personality revealed through checking off auditions
jokes.punchline: Leslie's neurotic need for completed tasks and check marks
jokes.explanation: Strong character work - this is very Leslie Knope behavior and sets up her Type-A personality

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I need to see the completed audition forms with all signatures and initials. Every single box checked. Every. Single. Box. Do you understand? I will literally not be able to sleep tonight if there's even one blank space. I'll just lie there, staring at the ceiling, thinking about that unchecked box. My eye will twitch. I'll start muttering to myself. By morning, I'll have reorganized my entire apartment by the Dewey Decimal System just to distract myself from the existential dread of administrative incompleteness.

jokes.text: Perd's slam poetry: "I am speaking the slam poem to you right now with my mouth"
jokes.punchline: Completely literal description of what he's doing instead of actual poetry
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Perd's character - overly literal news anchor who states obvious things

dialogue.speaker: Perd
dialogue.line: I am now reading poetry. I am holding a piece of paper. The paper has words on it. I am looking at the words. My eyes are moving from left to right. I am now moving my mouth. Sounds are coming out of my mouth. Some people are listening to the sounds. They are sitting in chairs. The chairs have legs. I am standing. My legs are also below me. I am wearing shoes on my feet.

jokes.text: "I cannot tell the difference between what's supposed to be slam poetry and what's just him talking"
jokes.punchline: Recognition that his 'poetry' is indistinguishable from his regular speech
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that extends the Perd joke logically

dialogue.speaker: Perd Hapley
dialogue.line: I have prepared a poem for you all. It is called 'The Winds of Change.' The winds of change are blowing through our fair city. They bring with them the promise of tomorrow. And also some allergies.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry Gergich
dialogue.line: That's... that's just the news.

dialogue.speaker: Perd Hapley
dialogue.line: Yes. Yes, it is.

jokes.text: "My Second Slam Poem by Perd Hapley, by Perd Hapley"
jokes.punchline: Redundant attribution showing his confusion about how titles work
jokes.explanation: Continues Perd's character trait of stating things redundantly and literally

dialogue.speaker: Perd Hapley
dialogue.line: I'd like to read you my second poem. It's called 'Poem: A Poem About Poems.'

dialogue.speaker: Perd Hapley
dialogue.line: A poem, by Perd Hapley.

jokes.text: Andy confusing vitamins: "Is that the Fred one or the Barney one?"
jokes.setup: Leslie tells Andy to take vitamin C
jokes.punchline: Andy thinks vitamins are named after Flintstones characters
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - childlike confusion delivered with complete sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You should take vitamin C.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Vitamin C? Like, Vitamin Barney? Vitamin Wilma?

jokes.text: "Pawnee is like a breeding ground for disease due to our poor hygiene habits and raccoon density"
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact mention of 'raccoon density' as a disease factor
jokes.explanation: Great world-building humor - absurd detail about Pawnee delivered as serious epidemiological fact

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: And that's why we need to account for raccoon density in our flu prevention protocol. It's a well-documented vector.

jokes.text: "We used to crown Miss Influenza every year. The CDC called the pageant 'ethically reprehensible'"
jokes.punchline: Pageant celebrating disease that horrified health authorities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - takes Pawnee's backwards nature to perfect extreme with CDC involvement

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We're throwing a pageant celebrating a disease that horrified health authorities across the country.

jokes.text: Larry quarantined in a tent: "The tent is your home now, Larry. We already forwarded your mail"
jokes.setup: Larry feels sick and wants to work from home
jokes.punchline: Leslie has literally banished him to a tent and redirected his mail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Leslie's germaphobia and typical Larry mistreatment

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Larry, you can't work from home. You'll contaminate the office. I've set up a tent in the parking lot for you.

dialogue.speaker: Larry
dialogue.line: A tent? Leslie, I have the flu, not leprosy.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I've also redirected all your mail there. It's safer this way.

jokes.text: "Chippmunks. Because of the animal? No, that's too simple"
jokes.punchline: Dismissing the obvious connection as too simple
jokes.explanation: Extends the absurdity logically

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Why is the fan called 'The Breeze'? That's so obvious!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown Character
dialogue.line: It literally just blows air around.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: No, no, no. There's got to be something deeper. Some kind of hidden meaning or clever wordplay.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown Character
dialogue.line: It's a fan. It makes a breeze.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Too simple. There's no way that's actually it.

jokes.text: Chipp McCapp's song lyrics: "She don't care about no fancy trends / She's just a mom from old South Bend"
jokes.punchline: Painfully generic country music lyrics
jokes.explanation: Generic parody of country music clichés

dialogue.speaker: Chipp McCapp
dialogue.line: My dog ran away and my truck broke down, my wife left me in this one-horse town, I lost my job at the factory, now all I got is this bottle and me.

jokes.text: Tom's sommelier recruitment strategy: "People that convince other people to buy expensive stuff they don't need. Those are the real heroes"
jokes.setup: Explaining why he wants to hire a sommelier
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate description of sales people as 'heroes'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom perspective - entrepreneurial cynicism delivered earnestly

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Look, a sommelier is just a fancy word for a wine salesman. And salesmen? They're heroes. They're out there every day, grinding, hustling, making things happen. People don't respect sales enough. A sommelier walks into a room, suddenly everyone thinks he's sophisticated. That's the power of sales, baby. That's why I want to hire one.

jokes.text: Ben's wine knowledge: "I studied botany for three years at Purdue University. Go, Boilermakers!"
jokes.punchline: His qualification is unrelated botany study plus random school spirit
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ben's earnest nerdiness

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I know a lot about wine. I took a course in plant biology in college, and I went to Indiana University, so...

jokes.text: Tom's guest list complaints: "I started with two cool people and I ended up with a bunch of grandpas"
jokes.setup: Everyone wanting to join the wine trip
jokes.punchline: Tom's horror at his cool trip being invaded by the uncool
jokes.explanation: Captures Tom's image consciousness well

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: This wine trip is going to be amazing. Just me, some beautiful vineyards, and some of the finest wines in the world.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh, that sounds wonderful! Can I come?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Yeah, that does sound pretty cool.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm in.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Road trip!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No. No, no, no. This is not a group activity. This is a solo journey for a sophisticated man of culture and means.

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Can I join?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh my God. No! Why would you... No!

jokes.text: "How would we embarrass you?" "You're already doing it. I begged you"
jokes.punchline: Tom's immediate exasperation that they're already embarrassing him
jokes.explanation: Good structure and economy, captures Tom's frustration

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa. You're already embarrassing me and we haven't even started yet.

jokes.text: Leslie's wish list: "Bob Dylan's friends"
jokes.punchline: Vague hope that Dylan's associates might help
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie optimism - unrealistic but hopeful networking

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We could always call Dylan's people, see if any of his associates might want to headline.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: His associates? Leslie, Dylan's people are criminals and drug addicts.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yes, but they're very talented criminals and drug addicts.

jokes.text: Land Ho album title: "We'll Never Reunite"
jokes.punchline: On-the-nose album title stating their position
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup for later reunion attempt

dialogue.speaker: Unknown Character
dialogue.line: On-the-nose album title stating their position

jokes.text: Xander described as "the Bruno Mars of Indiana amateur wine tasting"
jokes.punchline: Overly specific comparison to a pop star in a niche field
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist comparison with perfect specificity

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Xander's got skills like Usher in the 2004 R&B landscape—smooth, precise, totally in command of his lane.

jokes.text: Xander's nasal preparation: "I pour saline water through my nose to clear the mucus from my smell centers"
jokes.punchline: Gross but probably real sommelier practice
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor based on real sommelier practices

dialogue.line: Gross but probably real sommelier practice

jokes.text: April's motivation: "I wanted to make fun of stupid people while I get drunk. My two true passions"
jokes.setup: Explaining why she wants to join the competition
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest admission of malicious intent
jokes.explanation: Perfect April line - deadpan honesty about her antisocial tendencies

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I want to join the competition so I can sabotage everyone else and watch them fail. It brings me joy.

jokes.text: April claiming to study at "The Wine Academy in Bordeaux"
jokes.punchline: Confidently lying about prestigious wine education
jokes.explanation: Good April moment - casual dishonesty delivered with confidence

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I went to the Culinary Institute of France for three years studying viticulture and oenology under the tutelage of the world's foremost wine experts. I also have a degree in French literature from the Sorbonne.

jokes.text: Craig paying with "debit rewards card" to prove wine knowledge
jokes.setup: Trying to prove he belongs at wine tasting
jokes.punchline: Offering mundane financial instrument as proof of sophistication
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Craig's misplaced priorities

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I belong here. I have a Roth IRA.

jokes.text: Ron's color spectrum drinking rule: "I don't drink alcohol from that portion of the color spectrum"
jokes.punchline: Arbitrary but specific rule about alcohol colors
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron line - arbitrary but logically stated personal rule that sounds almost reasonable

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I only drink alcohol that's clear or brown. I will not drink blueberry wine.

jokes.text: Andy's first aid confusion: "I know first aid. Or karate" / "That's not first aid" / "It is if you do it right"
jokes.punchline: Confusing violence with medical care
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity with perfect Andy logic

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Are you okay? Do you need medical attention?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm fine, I'm fine. Why does everyone keep asking me that?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Because you have a giant bruise on your face.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh, that's just from when I fell down the stairs at work.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's terrible! We need to get you to a hospital.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: No, no, I'm good. I'll just go punch myself a few more times. That usually helps.

jokes.text: Leslie's medicine request: "The Mariah-needs-to-sing-tonight stuff"
jokes.setup: Asking pharmacist for strong flu medicine
jokes.punchline: Reference to Mariah Carey's diva reputation and performance demands
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference that perfectly captures the intensity Leslie wants

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I need the strongest flu medicine you have. I have to be at a meeting tomorrow and I cannot miss it.

dialogue.speaker: Pharmacist
dialogue.line: This should do it, but you need to follow the instructions carefully.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Don't worry, I can handle it. I once organized an entire town festival while battling a stomach bug. I'm basically Mariah Carey.

dialogue.speaker: Pharmacist
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You know, a diva who demands perfection no matter what. Except instead of hitting high notes, I'm hitting deadlines.

jokes.text: Pharmacist's amateur diagnosis: "Could be aseptic meningitis. What is that? Is that bad? It'd mean you have, like, an hour to live. What? It's probably not that. Sorry, it's my first day"
jokes.punchline: Escalating from mild diagnosis to death sentence to casual dismissal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, captures incompetent medical advice fears

dialogue.speaker: Pharmacist
dialogue.line: Well, let's see here. You've got a slight case of the sniffles.

dialogue.speaker: Pharmacist
dialogue.line: Actually, wait. That's pneumonia. You're going to die.

dialogue.speaker: Pharmacist
dialogue.line: Oh never mind, I wasn't looking at your chart. You're fine. That'll be fifteen dollars.

jokes.text: Andy's candy math: holding one large candy and saying "This counts as one"
jokes.setup: Leslie said he could buy two candies
jokes.punchline: Choosing one enormous candy to game the system
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - technically following rules while completely subverting them

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, Leslie said I could buy two candies?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So if I buy one really huge candy, that counts as two, right? I'm gaming the system!

jokes.text: Ron's wine venting timer: "You may vent for as long as it takes me to drink this bottle of wine"
jokes.setup: Ben asking to vent about problems
jokes.punchline: Setting a drinking-based time limit for conversation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron line - practical, antisocial, and alcohol-focused solution to social obligation

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Can I vent to you about something?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Of course. I'm going to have a drink, and when it's gone, this conversation is over.

jokes.text: Ron's terrible counseling feedback: "You are a wonderful guy, and I admire many things about you. But you're a terrible person to discuss personal problems with"
jokes.punchline: Accurate assessment of Ron's counseling abilities delivered as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Ben being honest about Ron's limitations

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: My mom called me again about my dad. They're having issues.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Ben, you need to stop letting them pull you into their drama.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I know, but they're my parents. What am I supposed to do?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Tell them to work it out themselves. You're a grown man.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Ron, that's actually really good advice. You should be a therapist.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Absolutely not. I have no interest in other people's problems.

jokes.text: Ron's positive response: "Thank you, friend. That really means a lot to me"
jokes.setup: Being told he's terrible at personal counseling
jokes.punchline: Taking the criticism as a genuine compliment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response - genuinely pleased to be recognized as emotionally unavailable

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I don't take criticism well. But I'm working on it. In fact, I consider what you just said to be a genuine compliment, and I thank you for it.

jokes.text: April's wine description: "It's like a Charlie Brown Halloween special" because of pumpkin notes
jokes.punchline: Completely unexpected pop culture comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant April moment - unexpected reference that actually makes sense

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: This wine tastes like if a Kardashian was a fruit.

jokes.text: Craig's elaborate wine description: "dried robin's blood, old, dirty cashews from your grandfather's basement, and just a hint of a robot's bathwater"
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disturbing and impossible flavor notes
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation parody of pretentious wine descriptions

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I'm detecting notes of blackberry, oak, and a hint of vanilla.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Now I'm getting some leather, tobacco, and what appears to be a small child's scream.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: Is that... is that the sound of a marriage ending? Yes, I'm definitely tasting a failed marriage.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: And underneath all that, I'm picking up notes of regret, broken dreams, and possibly a ghost.

jokes.text: April's wine review: "That one sucks"
jokes.punchline: Brutally simple assessment after pretentious analysis
jokes.explanation: Perfect April deflation of pretension - maximum impact with minimal words

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: This wine has notes of blackberry, hints of oak, a subtle undertone of tobacco with just a whisper of vanilla on the finish.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: It tastes like wine.

jokes.text: Ben's wine origin: "This comes from your mother's butt. Possibly your father's butt. Oh, actually, wait, never mind, this is so embarrassing. This comes from John McEnroe's butt from the year 3055"
jokes.setup: Drunk Ben trying to do wine tasting
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and inappropriate "tasting notes"
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing drunk Ben completely losing it, perfect random specificity

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: This wine has notes of... blackberry, and oak, and also my ex-girlfriend's betrayal.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: This one tastes like regret. Regret and... is that plum?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Ooh, this one's got a real finish. It finishes with me crying in my car in the parking lot of a Home Depot.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: This wine pairs well with poor life choices and a complete lack of self-respect.

jokes.text: Ben's drunk phone call: "it's your me. It's wife. It's Leslie. Knope"
jokes.setup: Ben calling Leslie while very drunk
jokes.punchline: Completely garbled attempt at identifying himself
jokes.explanation: Good drunk dialogue, captures Ben's formal nature breaking down

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie? Leslie, hi, it's me. It's Ben. Ben Wyatt. I'm a man. I have a face. I work at the government. I'm calling you on a phone.

jokes.text: Leslie's random statements: 'my teeth are blue. Blueberries...' 'I was just saying to Ron that my dog's Jewish'
jokes.punchline: Stream of consciousness non-sequiturs
jokes.explanation: Callback to Ben's story but execution is just drunk rambling

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm calling to tell you that waffles are just pancakes that decided to get their life together. And also, I think Ben doesn't know that I reorganized his entire sock drawer by thread count.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Did you know that the word 'government' has 'govern' in it? Like, that's not a coincidence. Someone planned that. That person is a genius.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Anyway, I'm going to go eat a entire wheel of cheese and call it dinner. Love you. Bye!

jokes.text: "It's as dead as Stanley Kubrick. I miss them both so much, Ron"
jokes.punchline: Treating his phone and Stanley Kubrick as equally mournable losses
jokes.explanation: Good absurd comparison showing Ben's film nerd personality even while drunk

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: My phone just died. It's like when Stanley Kubrick died.

jokes.text: Ron's walking direction: "Home is that way" pointing completely wrong direction
jokes.setup: Offering to walk home with Ben
jokes.punchline: Confidently pointing in the wrong direction
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag, works but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I'll walk you home.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Oh, thanks Ron.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: It's this way.

jokes.text: Tom's Montreal Expo dating claim: "I picked up a little French-Canadian when I dated that Montreal Expo"
jokes.punchline: Claiming to date a baseball team member
jokes.explanation: Good Tom bragging with specific sports reference

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm dating a baseball player.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, I'm not actually dating him, but I told everyone at the wine thing that I was.

jokes.text: "don't act like you don't know the word 'thing'"
jokes.punchline: Calling out obvious fake confusion
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to pretentious behavior

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh, 'je ne sais quoi'? Really, Terrence? You don't know what? It's literally the name of the thing you're describing. That's not French, that's just giving up.

jokes.text: Craig's wine recommendations: "Something bold and dry, like David Sedaris"
jokes.punchline: Describing wine using personality traits of a humor writer
jokes.explanation: Creative and unexpected comparison that actually works

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: For a nice steak, you want a wine with some backbone. Something assertive, confident, maybe a little neurotic and self-deprecating. So basically, a wine that's like a humor writer.

jokes.text: "This is that wine" - connecting wine to movie murder scenes
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to evidence in murder scenes
jokes.explanation: Dark and unexpected wine description

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: This wine has notes of blackberry, plum, and a hint of... is that... blood? Yeah, this tastes like evidence from a murder scene.

jokes.text: Chipp's diva behavior with LunchStacks: "Cheese on top, or no one gets paid"
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous demands about sandwich construction
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity diva parody

dialogue.speaker: Chipp
dialogue.line: I need my sandwiches cut diagonally, not straight across. And the bread has to be toasted to exactly 347 degrees. The lettuce must be arranged in a counter-clockwise pattern, and each piece of cheese needs to be individually blessed by a spiritual healer before it touches the bread.

jokes.text: April's wine philosophy: "I think we can agree that all wine tastes the same. And if you spend more than $5 on wine, you are very stupid"
jokes.punchline: Completely dismissing entire wine culture in victory speech
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - winning by completely rejecting the premise of what she won

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Thank you. I want to say that wine is just old juice and anyone who pretends to like it is a liar. The 'notes' you taste are made up. That 'full body' you're experiencing is just expensive marketing. I'm going to use my platform as sommelier champion to tell people the truth: wine is bad and we should all just drink beer or vodka like normal people.

jokes.text: April's Eddie Murphy acceptance speech: "Norbit, uh, Pluto Nash, all the Klumps"
jokes.punchline: Thanking Eddie Murphy's worst movies
jokes.explanation: Perfect April absurdity - thanking terrible movies for wine expertise

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I want to thank Eddie Murphy's worst movies for inspiring me to be better.

jokes.text: Tom's offer negotiation: "I'll double whatever he's paying you" "$500,000 a year?" "I will pay you one-tenth of whatever he's paying you"
jokes.punchline: Immediately backing down when hearing actual amount
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tom's business limitations

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm gonna outbid him. How much is he offering?

dialogue.speaker: Xander
dialogue.line: Twelve thousand dollars.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm out.

jokes.text: "this wine bottle is all out of the wine"
jokes.punchline: Childlike description of being out of alcohol
jokes.explanation: Simple drunk dialogue, mildly amusing

dialogue.speaker: Unknown Character
dialogue.line: I'm out of alcohol. It's like my bottle broke its promises to me.

jokes.text: "Am I?" response to "Are you a ghost?" and philosophical time discussion
jokes.punchline: Philosophical non-answer to simple question
jokes.explanation: Good character introduction for mystical Ron Dunn

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I don't know what you want from me.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: A man is what he does, not what he says he'll do.

jokes.text: Chipp's casual city insult: "They sure ain't buying toothpaste. Bunch of dirty hicks"
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting his own fanbase
jokes.explanation: Shows Chipp's awful personality through casual bigotry

dialogue.speaker: Chipp
dialogue.line: Casually insulting his own fanbase

jokes.text: Chipp shooting paintball at his father: "If I want to shoot a paintball gun at my dad, I'm going to do it" followed by "Good shot, son. I'm proud of you"
jokes.punchline: Father praising the son who just shot him
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy showing dysfunctional family dynamic

dialogue.speaker: Chipp
dialogue.line: I want to shoot the gun!

dialogue.speaker: Father
dialogue.line: No, you're too young.

dialogue.speaker: Chipp
dialogue.line: But I WANT to! You're the worst father ever!

dialogue.speaker: Father
dialogue.line: Fine, here's the gun.

dialogue.speaker: Chipp
dialogue.line: I'm gonna shoot you!

dialogue.speaker: Father
dialogue.line: That's my boy!

jokes.text: Leslie's revenge: "when you shook my hand earlier, there was pee on my palms" followed by Andy: "You get pee-palm, too? Every time!"
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to get back at Chipp
jokes.punchline: Andy revealing this is a regular occurrence
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Andy's oblivious confirmation

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay, Chipp, you think you're so smart? Well, I'm going to get you back for this.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh no, not again. Last week she spent three hours planning an elaborate prank involving a fake permit application.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's because it works, Andy!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, but she does this like every other day. I've already had to create a spreadsheet to track her revenge plots.

jokes.text: Andy's pregnancy confusion: "I thought you were getting a dog" and dog petting gesture explanation
jokes.setup: Andy giving advice about Leslie handling bigger family
jokes.punchline: Complete misunderstanding of pregnancy announcement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - giving perfect advice based on completely wrong assumption

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Hey, Leslie, I know you're worried about how your family's gonna handle you moving up in your career. But trust me, big families adapt. My family's huge and we figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's really sweet, Andy. I'm actually pregnant.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: See? Your family's gonna be so excited when you tell them you got a promotion!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: No, Andy. I'm pregnant. With a baby.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Right, yeah, and that's gonna be great for your career trajectory. You're gonna crush it.

jokes.text: "She's gettin' a dog!" Andy announcing Leslie's pregnancy wrong to waitress
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastically spreading misinformation
jokes.explanation: Good callback maintaining Andy's confusion

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, so the baby's gonna be inside Ann the whole time? Like, living in there?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's incredible. So basically Ann's a spaceship now. She's literally carrying precious cargo.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Does the baby need, like, a seatbelt in there? Because that seems like a safety issue.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh! Oh! I know what we should do. We should talk to the baby every day so it knows our voices. I read that somewhere, or I'm pretty sure I did.

jokes.text: Ron Dunn's mystical wine knowledge: "The Wamapoke called it 'mattapiqua.' They believed it had mystical powers"
jokes.punchline: Obscure historical/mystical wine knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good character building for mystical Ron Dunn

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Blueberry wine. A bold choice. Most people don't understand the complexity of berry fermentation. The ancient Mesopotamians believed blueberries contained the tears of forgotten gods. Personally, I just appreciate that it pairs well with venison and solitude.

jokes.text: Role-playing therapy assignment: "I'll be your father. And, Ron, you take the role of Ben's anger"
jokes.setup: Offering to help with Ben's family issues
jokes.punchline: Assigning Ron to literally play an emotion
jokes.explanation: Good absurd therapy suggestion

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: Ron, I'm assigning you to play an emotion.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Leslie, emotions are a sign of weakness. I don't have them.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie Knope
dialogue.line: You're playing Anger.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I'm not angry.

jokes.text: Craig's emotional breakdown: "I'm going to go home, put on a Macy Gray album, and cry in the mirror!"
jokes.setup: Craig being upset about not getting the job
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic and specific depression plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect Craig drama with specific sad music choice

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I'm not getting that job. I can feel it. And you know what? I'm fine with it. I'm going to go home, I'm going to get a bottle of the cheapest scotch they have, I'm going to pour it into a glass that still has yesterday's lipstick on it, I'm going to get into bed, I'm going to scroll through pictures of my ex-girlfriend's vacation, and I'm going to cry until my eyes are swollen shut.

jokes.text: Other Ron volunteering to play Ben's anger while Ron Dunn plays his father
jokes.punchline: Ron Swanson matter-of-factly agreeing to embody anger
jokes.explanation: Great two-Ron dynamic - Swanson's practical approach to hippie therapy concepts

dialogue.speaker: Ron Dunn
dialogue.line: What if we did some role-play therapy? You embody anger, and I'll try to talk you down.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: Fine. I'm angry.

jokes.text: Craig's intensity promise: "I'll bring it down 1,000 notches if I have to!"
jokes.setup: Being told he needs to tone it down
jokes.punchline: Extremely intense declaration about being less intense
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment - being intense about not being intense

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I will tone it down. I will be LESS INTENSE. I will speak in a measured, calm, and reasonable manner AT ALL TIMES. I WILL NOT RAISE MY VOICE. I WILL NOT USE EXCESSIVE PUNCTUATION OR DRAMATIC GESTURES. I WILL SIT QUIETLY AND THINK PEACEFUL THOUGHTS. I WILL MEDITATE ON THE NATURE OF TRANQUILITY AND ZEN-LIKE CALM. NAMASTE.

jokes.text: Andy's SAT strategy: "Same way I got a perfect score on the SATs. Broken Scantron machine"
jokes.setup: Explaining how to convince Scott Tanner
jokes.punchline: Revealing his academic success was accidental
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - accidental success he thinks was strategic

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You just gotta tell him that his academic success was actually an accident. Like, he didn't mean to get good grades, it just happened.

jokes.text: The elaborate Karate Chop Master song performance and Scott joining in
jokes.punchline: Getting famous musician to participate in silly karate song
jokes.explanation: Extended physical comedy sequence, heartwarming but not exceptionally funny

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Karate! Hi-YAH! Karate! Hi-YAH!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: It's time to learn karate, it's time to learn karate, it's time to learn karate from me!

jokes.text: Customer's wine order: 'I'm having fish, so maybe a full-bodied red? And bring some ice cubes. I like ice cubes in my red wine.'
jokes.punchline: Deliberately wrong wine choices to test reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe test scenario but functional

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: I'm going to deliberately give you wrong wine pairings to see how you react.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Wait, what?

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: For a fish course, I'm recommending this heavy Cabernet Sauvignon.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: That's... that's completely wrong.

dialogue.speaker: Craig
dialogue.line: And for dessert, a dry, tannic Barolo.

dialogue.speaker: Customer
dialogue.line: Who are you?

jokes.text: Tom's embarrassed realization about private phone calls being overheard
jokes.punchline: Realizes private business was public
jokes.explanation: Standard embarrassment humor without much craft

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait, so Craig could hear everything we were saying in that office?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yeah, the walls are basically paper thin.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Oh my God. Oh my God, no. Last week I was in there telling Ben about my entire business plan for Rent-A-Swag.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Your what now?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Exactly! Craig heard it first!

jokes.text: Ben's spirit animal skepticism vs. revelation: "Because it doesn't exist because that's nonsense because you're an adult" then "Baby snow owl"
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself with specific spirit animal
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Ben's rational nature breaking down

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: I don't believe in spirit animals. That's not based in any kind of science or logic.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: My spirit animal is the bald eagle.

jokes.text: Andy's continued dog confusion: "You're gonna need a cage, a water bowl... You have a good vet? Tick medicine"
jokes.setup: Leslie going home to tell Ben about pregnancy
jokes.punchline: Andy still thinking she's getting a dog and giving pet advice
jokes.explanation: Good callback maintaining Andy's confusion throughout episode

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm going home to tell Ben some big news.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh my god, you're getting a dog! That's so exciting! You're gonna love it. Pro tip: get the biggest bag of dog food you can find. It lasts way longer, and dogs don't care about freshness.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Andy, I'm not getting a dog.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh, you'll change your mind. Everyone does. Also, name it something cool like Thunderbolt or Rape Ape.

jokes.text: Ron Dunn's philosophical life advice ending with "will you fly?"
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top mystical metaphor about emerging from cocoon
jokes.explanation: Typical new age spiritual advice parody

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: You are like a caterpillar in a cocoon. And soon, you will emerge as a beautiful butterfly and spread your wings and fly away... from government.

jokes.text: Ben's honest reaction: "Yeah, I don't feel any better. I'm still angry. Damn it"
jokes.punchline: Admitting the therapy completely failed
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the mystical healing sequence

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Yeah, so that didn't work at all.

tmdb_overview: Leslie and Ben run a charity auction to raise funds for the unity concert. April steps in to manage Andy's schedule, while Donna asks Ron for help with her ex-boyfriend.
insight: Absurdist escalation carries 67 jokes through a mid-tier flu episode despite weak standouts.
